 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t11","title":"fcp.arxiv: Author information and publication history","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Author information and publication history (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide author-centric discovery:
- list papers by author name
- fetch author publication history (bounded)
- optional: infer topics/categories distribution

## Scope
- Search papers by author filter.
- Aggregate author stats:
  - papers per category
  - publication timeline
- Optional: disambiguation hints (best-effort).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.author.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Output bounded:
  - max papers returned
  - stable ordering

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.author.papers.list`
  - `arxiv.author.stats`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - author query encoding
  - bounds enforcement

## Acceptance criteria
- Author history queries are reliable, bounded, and deterministic.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t690","title":"TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-host Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-host` core behaviors (supervision, sandboxing, policy enforcement, logs) with deterministic tests and mocks.

## Scope
Unit tests for the Gateway/Orchestrator implementation.

## Test Categories

### Connector Supervisor
- [ ] Connector process spawning
- [ ] Process termination handling
- [ ] Health monitoring
- [ ] Restart policy enforcement
- [ ] Resource limit enforcement
- [ ] Crash detection and recovery

### IPC Hub
- [ ] Message routing correctness
- [ ] Request/response correlation
- [ ] Timeout handling
- [ ] Event fan-out
- [ ] Connection lifecycle

### Zone Enforcer
- [ ] Zone configuration loading
- [ ] Request zone validation
- [ ] Cross-zone policy enforcement
- [ ] Data flow control

### Capability Verifier
- [ ] Token parsing
- [ ] Signature verification
- [ ] Capability scope checking
- [ ] Constraint enforcement
- [ ] Expiration handling

### Sandbox Manager
- [ ] Filesystem isolation setup
- [ ] Network filtering rules
- [ ] Configuration generation

### Agent API
- [ ] HTTP endpoint routing
- [ ] WebSocket handling
- [ ] Request validation
- [ ] Response formatting
- [ ] Error handling

### Connector Registry
- [ ] Manifest parsing
- [ ] Signature verification
- [ ] Version comparison
- [ ] Discovery

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock connector processes
- Mock IPC communications
- Inject various failure modes

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Dependencies
- fcp-core (must be stable first)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-t77","title":"fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling","description":"# fcp.jira: Comment and attachment handling (FCP2)

## Goal
Add first-class support for Jira issue **comments** and **attachments**, including safe upload/download flows that preserve FCP2 security invariants.

Comments and attachments are high-leverage for real workflows, but they also carry:
- PII risk
- large binary payload risk
- data exfil risk

## Scope
### Comments
- List comments for an issue (pagination).
- Create/update/delete comments (with visibility rules when supported).
- Convert between:
  - Jira Cloud ADF (Atlassian Document Format)
  - a canonical connector representation (e.g., markdown + structured spans)

### Attachments
- List attachments for an issue (metadata only).
- Download attachment content (streamed; size limits; hash).
- Upload attachment (streamed; content-type allowlist; filename sanitization).
- Delete attachment (dangerous; approval-gated).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.comments.read`
- `jira.comments.write`
- `jira.attachments.read`
- `jira.attachments.write` (dangerous: data egress into Jira)
- `jira.attachments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Jira base hostname and any attachment host if distinct.
- No secrets in logs.
- **Binary safety**:
  - enforce max download size; abort early
  - enforce max upload size; abort early
  - compute content hash for receipts/audit evidence
- **Taint model**:
  - comment bodies and attachment filenames are tainted inputs
  - do not allow tainted text to trigger dangerous operations without approval

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.comment.list`
  - `jira.comment.create`
  - `jira.comment.update`
  - `jira.comment.delete`
  - `jira.attachment.list`
  - `jira.attachment.download`
  - `jira.attachment.upload`
  - `jira.attachment.delete`
- Streaming download/upload helpers compatible with sandboxed IO.

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- ADF ↔ markdown conversion golden tests.
- Upload tests:
  - filename normalization
  - content-type allowlist
  - size limits
- Download tests:
  - streaming behavior
  - early abort on oversized content
  - hash correctness

### Compliance
- Any write/delete emits OperationReceipt + AuditEvent with:
  - issue key
  - comment id / attachment id
  - content hash (never raw content)

## Acceptance criteria
- Comments round-trip through conversion without losing critical structure.
- Attachment IO is streaming + bounded (no unbounded buffering).
- Dangerous operations are capability + (policy) approval gated and audited.

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-t9nj","title":"FIX: connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime test arch normalization","description":"## Bug Summary

The test `connector_target_from_env_matches_runtime` was failing because it compared `ConnectorTarget::from_env().arch` directly with `std::env::consts::ARCH`.

However, `ConnectorTarget::from_env()` intentionally normalizes architecture names for OCI/Docker compatibility:
- `x86_64` → `amd64`
- `aarch64` → `arm64`

## Fix

Updated the test to expect the normalized arch value by using the same mapping logic that `from_env()` uses.

## Files Changed

- `crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs`: Fixed test assertion to account for arch normalization

## Verification

- All 48 fcp-registry tests pass
- Full workspace tests pass
- No clippy warnings introduced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:29:43.400900968-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:29:59.155461943-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T01:29:59.155461943-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["registry","testing"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-thy","title":"fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving","description":"# fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core SMS/MMS capabilities:
- send outbound SMS (and MMS when enabled)
- read message status/history
- support inbound SMS via webhook-driven event flow (ingest handled in `flywheel_connectors-ugp`)

## Scope
### Outbound
- Send SMS to a single destination.
- Optional: bulk send with batching + rate limiting.
- Support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate sends.

### Status / history
- Get message details by SID.
- List messages with filters (date range, to/from).

### Media (MMS)
- List/download media attachments (bounded + host pinned).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.sms.send`
- `twilio.sms.read`
- `twilio.sms.media.read`

Policy guidance:
- Outbound send is a real-world side effect; many orgs require approval for certain destinations.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s) and explicit media hosts.
- For sends:
  - receipts + audit events with message SID + to/from hashes (never raw phone numbers in logs)
  - strict log redaction for phone numbers and message bodies
- Taint model:
  - inbound message bodies are tainted by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.sms.send`
  - `twilio.sms.get`
  - `twilio.sms.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.download`
- Idempotency strategy:
  - stable client idempotency key → dedupe cache/state

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send request formation
  - idempotency behavior
  - status polling
  - media download size limits
  - secret/PII redaction in logs

## Acceptance criteria
- Duplicate sends are mechanically prevented via idempotency.
- Logs and receipts never leak phone numbers or message bodies.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","title":"TEST: Revocation System Unit/Integration Tests","description":"## Overview

Comprehensive test suite for Revocation System (i4zm). This is CRITICAL security testing.

## Test Categories

### 1. RevocationObject Tests
- **Creation**: Valid revocation with owner signature
- **Signature validation**: Only owner can sign revocations
- **Scope variants**: Test all RevocationScope types
- **Effective timing**: effective_at in past/present/future
- **Expiry handling**: Temporary vs permanent revocations

### 2. RevocationEvent Chain Tests
- **Chain integrity**: Hash links verified
- **Monotonic seq**: seq always increases
- **Chain traversal**: Full history retrievable
- **Concurrent additions**: No forks in chain

### 3. RevocationHead Tests
- **Quorum validation**: Requires N-of-M signatures
- **Freshness comparison**: head_seq comparison works
- **Head updates**: New events create new heads
- **Cross-zone heads**: Separate chains per zone

### 4. RevocationRegistry Tests
- **Bloom filter accuracy**: No false negatives
- **is_revoked() correctness**: True positives work
- **Fast path performance**: Bloom filter ~O(1)
- **Registry persistence**: Survives restart
- **Registry rebuild**: Reconstruct from chain

### 5. Enforcement Point Tests
- **Capability verification**: Revoked tokens rejected
- **Freshness check**: Stale revocation state detected
- **All scopes enforced**: Each scope type enforced correctly
- **Timing edge cases**: effective_at boundary conditions

### 6. Propagation Tests
- **Gossip priority**: Revocations propagate quickly
- **Network partition**: Revocations merge after heal
- **Duplicate handling**: Same revocation received twice
- **Order independence**: Out-of-order delivery handled

### 7. Use Case Tests
- **Capability revocation**: Token revoke → immediate rejection
- **IssuerKey revocation**: Node can't mint new tokens
- **NodeAttestation revocation**: Complete device removal
- **ZoneKey revocation**: Forces key rotation
- **ConnectorBinary revocation**: Binary hash blocked

## Logging Requirements

All tests emit structured JSON:
```json
{
  "test": "test_revocation_enforcement",
  "scope": "Capability",
  "revoked_object_id": "objectid:...",
  "enforcement_point": "verify_capability_token",
  "result": "rejected_as_expected",
  "timing_us": 45
}
```

## Security-Focused Tests

- **Replay attacks**: Old revocation can't un-revoke
- **Signature bypass**: Unsigned revocations rejected
- **Scope escalation**: Capability scope can't revoke zone key
- **Clock attacks**: Manipulated timestamps detected

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All RevocationScope variants tested
- [ ] Chain integrity verified
- [ ] Quorum signatures tested
- [ ] All enforcement points verified
- [ ] Propagation tested
- [ ] Use cases validated
- [ ] Security tests comprehensive
- [ ] Structured JSON logging in all tests","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:45:10.987703687-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T04:47:44.198369215-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T04:47:44.198369215-05:00","close_reason":"Duplicate of 36x3 which is already comprehensive","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:45:18.043148479-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-u133","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T04:45:18.301820265-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-u4ej","title":"TEST: DeviceProfile Unit Tests (Capability Reporting, Resource Constraints, Fitness)","description":"# TEST: DeviceProfile Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate DeviceProfile capability reporting and resource constraints behavior used by the execution planner.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for DeviceProfile type and execution fitness calculations.

## Test Categories

### Capability Reporting
- [ ] Profile correctly reports CPU cores
- [ ] Profile correctly reports memory
- [ ] Profile correctly reports storage
- [ ] Profile correctly reports network capability
- [ ] Profile correctly reports sandbox support (OS/WASI)

### Resource Constraints
- [ ] Constraint checking for CPU-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for memory-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for storage-bound connectors
- [ ] Constraint checking for network-dependent connectors
- [ ] Combined constraint validation

### Execution Fitness
- [ ] Fitness score calculation is deterministic
- [ ] Higher-spec device scores higher
- [ ] Missing capabilities result in zero fitness
- [ ] Fitness ranking orders devices correctly
- [ ] Tie-breaking uses node_id for determinism

### Profile Serialization
- [ ] Round-trip serialization preserves all fields
- [ ] Schema hash is stable
- [ ] Malformed profile data is rejected

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/device_profile/`
- `device_profile_full.cbor`
- `device_profile_minimal.cbor`
- `fitness_calculation_vectors.json`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_execution_fitness",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "device_profile": {...},
  "connector_requirements": {...},
  "fitness_score": 0.85,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All DeviceProfile fields tested
- Fitness calculation has golden vectors
- No panics on malformed profiles
